steps.title: Identify the type of growth
steps.content: Harper starts with $110$ and earns $2.7\%$ more each year. One year later the amount is $110(1.027)=112.97$. Use the values at $x=0$ and $x=1$ directly.

steps.title: Write the model
steps.content: A $2.7\%$ increase means the amount is multiplied by $$1+0.027=1.027$$ each year. Starting from $110$, the model is $$A(t)=110(1.027)^t.$$ Because $t$ is in the exponent, the model is exponential, not linear.

steps.title: Determine increasing or decreasing
steps.content:
The base $1.027 \gt 1$, so $A(t)$ grows as $t$ increases. The model is increasing exponential.
  • Choice A — Decreasing exponential The base exceeds 1, so the function increases, not decreases.
  • Choice B — Decreasing linear Growth is proportional (multiplicative), not additive, so it is not linear and it is not decreasing.
  • Choice C — Increasing exponential Multiplying by $1.027$ each year produces exponential growth. That is the correct answer.
  • Choice D — Increasing linear A fixed percentage applied to a changing balance produces exponential, not linear, growth.
